fields.description:
## Why
Today rebar has TWO session resolvers with DIFFERENT precedence chains:
`session_log._resolve_session_fp` (`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> REBAR_SESSION_ID ->
SESSION_ID -> None`) and `transition_close._resolve_session` (`REBAR_SESSION_ID ->
SESSION_ID -> git HEAD -> "unknown"`). The latter OMITS `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, so in a
real Claude Code session the FORCE_CLOSE audit comment falls through to git HEAD — a value
that changes every commit and is not a session id. This is a latent bug, and the two must
converge onto ONE shared resolver before anything else in the epic builds on session
resolution.

## What
Introduce one shared `resolve_session_id()` helper in a NEW module
`src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py`, implementing a single **data-driven, ordered** var
list so new harness vars are a one-line add:

    _SESSION_ID_VARS = ["REBAR_SESSION_ID", "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID", "SESSION_ID"]

Precedence rationale: the explicit, rebar-owned `REBAR_SESSION_ID` wins first (an
operator/hook override is authoritative), then the native harness var
`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, then the ambient `SESSION_ID`, else `None`.

- **Empty-string handling (DECISION):** a var whose value is empty / whitespace-only is
  treated as ABSENT (skipped), matching the existing falsy-`or`-chain semantics. Only the
  first *non-empty* var wins; all-empty / all-absent -> `None`.
- **Refactor both call sites onto it:**
  - `session_log._resolve_session_fp()` -> `return resolve_session_id()`. This
    **intentionally inverts** session_log's current precedence (today `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`
    before `REBAR_SESSION_ID`; now `REBAR_SESSION_ID` first). Impact is limited to sessions
    that set BOTH vars (rare); the unified explicit-rebar-first contract is deliberate and is
    documented in `docs/config.md`.
  - `transition_close._resolve_session(tracker)` -> `resolve_session_id() or
    _short_head(tracker) or "unknown"`. The shared resolver returns `None` on absence; the
    git-HEAD / `"unknown"` fallback stays LOCAL to this call site (a cosmetic non-empty
    audit string). This FIXES THE BUG: `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is now captured at the
    FORCE_CLOSE audit site.
- **AC4 scope (DECISION):** "never returns git HEAD" constrains the **return value of
  `resolve_session_id()` ONLY**. The FORCE_CLOSE audit comment MAY still render short HEAD
  via the documented local cosmetic fallback — that is out of scope for the shared resolver.
- **Deferred to S5 (c557):** the OSS harness vars `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` / `CODEX_THREAD_ID`
  are added to the list by story `privy-eloquent-nauplius`, which owns verifying their
  canonical names + harness provenance. S1 ships the extensible list with the three vars
  above only.
- Update the existing `_resolve_session` unit tests in `tests/unit/test_completion_gate.py`
  (~lines 477-504) that call the private helper, so they reflect the delegation.

## Scope
- IN: new `src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py` shared helper + ordered list; refactor of the
  two existing resolvers; unit tests for precedence / empty-string / all-absent + a
  call-site wiring test proving `transition_close` captures `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`;
  overwrite the stale session-provenance prose in `docs/config.md`.
- OUT: recording the id on claims (S2 / 68ef); `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`/`CODEX_THREAD_ID` +
  harness provenance (S5 / c557); reducer / read / hook work (later stories).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resolve_session_id()` returns the first NON-EMPTY var in order
      `REBAR_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID`, else `None`.
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_session_id.py -q`.
- [ ] An empty-string / whitespace-only value is skipped (treated as absent), verified by a
      dedicated test case.
- [ ] `resolve_session_id()` NEVER returns git HEAD (its var list contains no git call).
      Proof: source review + a test asserting `None` when all vars are unset.
- [ ] `session_log._resolve_session_fp` and `transition_close._resolve_session` BOTH
      delegate to the shared helper (no duplicate env chains).
      Proof: `grep -n "resolve_session_id" src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`.
- [ ] FORCE_CLOSE audit captures `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` when set (the bug fix).
      Proof: a test that sets ONLY `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, calls
      `transition_close._resolve_session(tracker)`, and asserts it returns that value (not HEAD).
- [ ] Existing `test_completion_gate.py` `_resolve_session` tests updated and green.
- [ ] `docs/config.md` session-provenance section rewritten to the unified ordered contract
      (including the precedence-inversion note); no stale OLD-precedence prose remains.
      Proof: `grep -n "CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID" docs/config.md`.

## Depends on
- (none — foundation story)
